key_insight: A physics-informed residual deep learning model can predict hot deformation flow stress and dynamic recrystallization in a Mo-rich b1+b2 titanium alloy with high accuracy while preserving constitutive physics.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable thermomechanical process design for titanium alloys by capturing strain hardening, softening, and recrystallization effects across broad temperature and strain-rate conditions.
method: Stacked Residual Physics-Informed Neural Networks (STAR-PINNs) trained on flow stress data from 800�b71050 �b0C and 0.01�b710 s�b9 strain rates
Enhanced STAR-PINN enforced thermomechanical constitutive constraints via automatic differentiation
DRX-Aware STAR-PINN added dual outputs, JMAK-Avrami regularization, DRX saturation constraints, and Arrhenius consistency
evidence_signal: The DRX-Aware model achieved RMSE = 11.69 MPa, MAE = 4.83 MPa, R�b2 = 0.9850, and cross-validated RMSE = 12.47 �b1 0.26 MPa, while reproducing flow curves and DRX kinetics.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of experimental training data, and the added physics constraints/latent fusion may be sensitive to parameter tuning outside the tested processing window.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Residual Deep Learning for Constitutive Modeling of Hot Deformation and Dynamic Recrystallization in a Mo-Rich $α+β$ Titanium Alloy
